meaning: adj.不道德的,无原则的,不合人道的
examples: Unprincipled accommodation will do harm to him.
It is a market where people can be very unprincipled and unpleasant.
He never gets involved in unprincipled disputes.
"Some of the things we are going to be introducing seem to be unprincipled, " he added.
Negotiating a deal by splitting the difference in bargaining positions is tempting but unprincipled.
